general: Those born in the Year of the Rabbit will encounter the influence of "Breaking Tai Sui" in the Year of the Horse, meaning they will offend Tai Sui. "Breaking" signifies the decline of interpersonal relationships, indicating that in the new year, misunderstandings may lead to awkward conversations with partners, friends, or business associates. This kind of damage doesn't happen instantly, but rather is the result of long-term accumulated dissatisfaction leading to a deterioration in relationships, thus requiring better communication to resolve disagreements. Although the influence of Breaking Tai Sui is not as strong as one's birth year or clashing with Tai Sui, the Year of the Horse is still a year of offending Tai Sui. It's suitable to engage in celebratory activities such as marriage, childbirth, property purchase, or starting a business to proactively promote change and reduce its negative impact. It's worth noting that the Year of the Horse also sees the auspicious star "Tianxi," symbolizing continuous good fortune. Couples planning marriage should actively pursue it, and married individuals may consider expanding their family. However, married individuals without plans to have children should be more self-disciplined, as the Year of the Horse also sees the star "Xianchi Peach Blossom," requiring those with partners to be constantly vigilant and resolutely resist external temptations to avoid getting involved in complicated love triangles. As for the other auspicious star, "Tian De," it symbolizes divine grace and signifies turning danger into safety. Coupled with the blessings of "Fu Xing" and "Ba Zuo," those born in the Year of the Rabbit will maintain an optimistic mindset in the Year of the Horse. Even when facing difficulties, they can rely on the support of benefactors and good fortune to overcome crises. Regarding inauspicious stars, those born in the Year of the Rabbit will inevitably encounter more complex interpersonal issues in the Year of the Horse. First, the star "Juan She" represents gossip and disputes. Although the year brings strong benefactor luck, it may also attract jealousy or gossip from petty people. Business people, in particular, need to be wary of malicious slander from colleagues or competitors, which could even lead to reputational damage and legal action. Furthermore, the Year of the Horse also brings "Jiao Sha," representing entangled relationships, and "Xian Chi Tao Hua," symbolizing fleeting romances. Those born in the Year of the Rabbit must pay attention to their words and actions, and act decisively when faced with protracted relationships, avoiding delays. In summary, facing various interpersonal challenges in the new year, although you will encounter auspicious stars and receive help from elders and benefactors, friction is likely to occur among peers and subordinates. In particular, you need to strengthen communication with old friends or business partners to prevent accumulated dissatisfaction from causing changes in relationships. Despite some impact on interpersonal relationships, fortunately, those born in the Year of the Rabbit can maintain a positive attitude and calmly deal with difficulties. It is recommended to wear dog-shaped zodiac ornaments in the Year of the Horse to utilize the power of "Six Harmonies" to enhance your fortune. It is also advisable to have a health check-up at the end of the Year of the Snake, perform the Tai Sui worship at the beginning of the Year of the Horse, and carry out small-scale renovations or repairs for your home. Those who enjoy horse racing or mahjong may consider placing bets to enhance their enjoyment, or try their luck with lottery tickets. However, some mild anxiety may arise in the Year of the Horse. It is recommended to take walks or short trips, and cultivate hobbies such as gardening or fishkeeping to cultivate a peaceful mind.

Due to the influence of the "Bing-Xin combination," with the new year approaching, special attention must be paid to the health of the respiratory system and lungs, especially for those with pre-existing bronchial allergies, who are more prone to persistent coughs. If you smoke, it is recommended to quit as soon as possible. Also, be aware of any allergens in your home environment. During the Year of the Horse, you also need to pay attention to various household matters, such as noise and leaks, and seek professional repair services promptly. However, it is best not to interfere excessively in the affairs of younger generations; allowing them to develop freely will help promote harmony and good family relationships.

In terms of wealth, the outlook is bright, and you are expected to profit in the stock market with the help of inspiration. Those who enjoy mahjong can also place bets appropriately for entertainment, with a high possibility of winning good fortune. However, you should avoid high-risk speculative activities to avoid losses. Due to the clash between water and fire, you should pay special attention to your heart and blood pressure. It is recommended to have a health check-up at the end of the Year of the Snake, and strengthening health management will prevent problems.

The new year will bring fresh development paths, encouraging people to explore different fields. For business professionals, this presents a good opportunity for career breakthroughs. Although direct financial returns may not be immediately apparent, the experience gained and connections built during this period will benefit future career development. However, be especially careful about gossip and disputes in the Year of the Horse, and guard against attacks from peers or competitors. Maintain a low profile in your interactions with others to avoid attracting attention and becoming a target. The new year will also bring some unexpected financial gains; as long as you avoid greed when investing, you can expect some returns.

When you reach forty (by the traditional Chinese age reckoning), this year will bring many changes, and your mindset may shift, leading to a desire to explore new possibilities. However, fortunes often fluctuate, making it easy to make mistakes in decision-making. Therefore, you must be extra cautious in both your career and investments. It is advisable to stay in familiar areas and continue learning and improving yourself, waiting until you are forty-two when your fortunes stabilize and your direction becomes clearer before actively moving forward. In addition, during the Year of the Horse, you should pay more attention to your own health and the health of your elders. Consider spending some time on minor home renovations and maintenance, or replacing old furniture to improve the family's fortune.

Throughout the year, you'll experience smooth sailing with helpful mentors and elders. Leveraging their social networks can lead to new developments, which will ultimately benefit your career growth in the long run. However, you may experience significant emotional fluctuations during the Year of the Horse, easily leading to overthinking due to work pressure. While you are capable of handling these difficulties, it's advisable to adjust your mindset and face them calmly. The new year also brings romantic opportunities. Singles should pay attention to those around them, as they may encounter someone they like. Married individuals, however, need to resist external temptations to avoid love triangles and getting themselves into trouble.

A positive learning attitude enhances focus and self-discipline, which is advisable for those planning to study abroad. However, young people born in the Year of the Rabbit are increasingly expressing their opinions, which may lead to friction with family and elders. Therefore, they should spend more time communicating and seeking consensus through open and honest dialogue.

Children in the year of Gui are generally quite well-behaved and intelligent, and perform well in their studies, so they are doted on by their elders and teachers. Health improved after the snake year, however parents still need to be aware of possible tracheal or skin allergies in their children. Therefore, attention must be paid to the surrounding air quality and checked for everyday use, such as shower gel, sheets and bedding, for irritating ingredients, and it is recommended to choose organic products to reduce the probability of allergies.

general: Those born in the Year of the Rabbit will face a "Breaking Tai Sui" situation when entering the Year of the Horse, meaning they will offend Tai Sui. "Breaking" signifies the deterioration of interpersonal relationships. In the new year, misunderstandings are likely to cause discord between partners, friends, or business associates. This damage to relationships doesn't happen suddenly but is the result of long-term accumulated dissatisfaction, thus requiring more communication to resolve differences. Although the influence of Breaking Tai Sui is relatively smaller than that of one's birth year or clashing with Tai Sui, the Year of the Horse is still considered an auspicious year for offending Tai Sui. It's suitable for celebratory events such as marriage, having children, buying property, or starting a business, as this can proactively welcome change and minimize its negative impact. Furthermore, the Year of the Horse brings the auspicious star "Tianxi," symbolizing frequent happy events. Couples planning marriage may consider taking action, and married individuals can also consider expanding their family. However, married individuals without plans to have children should be extra cautious, as the Year of the Horse also brings the "Xianchi Peach Blossom" star. Those with partners need to be vigilant, resolutely rejecting external temptations and avoiding getting involved in complicated love triangles. As for the other auspicious star, "Tian De," it symbolizes the virtues of heaven, implying turning danger into safety and gentle joy. Coupled with the presence of "Fu Xing" and "Ba Zuo," representing prosperous fortune, those born in the Year of the Rabbit can maintain a positive attitude in the Year of the Horse, and even when facing difficulties, they can rely on the help of benefactors and good fortune to turn danger into safety. Regarding the inauspicious stars, those born in the Year of the Rabbit will inevitably encounter relatively complex interpersonal problems in the Year of the Horse. First, "Juan She," as its name suggests, refers to verbal disputes. Although the power of benefactors is quite abundant in the new year, it is also easy to attract the jealousy or gossip of petty people. Especially for business people, they should be wary of malicious slander from colleagues or competitors, which may even affect their reputation and require legal action. In addition, the Year of the Horse will also face the "Jiao Sha," which indicates ambiguous relationships, and the "Xian Chi Tao Hua," symbolizing ambiguous fate. Those born in the Year of the Rabbit must pay extra attention to their words and actions, and make decisive decisions when faced with stagnant relationships, avoiding procrastination. In general, interpersonal relationships will become extreme in the new year. On the one hand, with the blessing of auspicious stars, the support of elders and benefactors will be plentiful. However, friction is likely to occur among peers and subordinates, especially with old friends or business partners, requiring more communication to avoid the deterioration of relationships due to long-term accumulated dissatisfaction. Although interpersonal relationships will encounter some challenges, fortunately, those born in the Year of the Rabbit will maintain a positive attitude and be able to calmly deal with difficulties. It is recommended to wear dog-shaped zodiac ornaments during the Year of the Horse to enhance personal fortune through the power of "six harmonies." It is also advisable to check one's health at the end of the Year of the Snake, prepare to worship Tai Sui at the beginning of the Year of the Horse, and carry out small-scale renovations and repairs at home. Thorough preparation in all matters can turn danger into safety.
career: "Tian De" represents virtuous conduct bestowed by Heaven. With the blessing of this auspicious star in the Year of the Horse, one is bound to receive support from benefactors. Furthermore, the "Fu Xing" and "Ba Zuo" stars, symbolizing luck and fortune, will bring smooth sailing to the career development of those in employment, especially those working in large organizations, whose performance will be particularly outstanding, and they are very likely to be promoted by their superiors and receive a corresponding salary adjustment. Those engaged in self-media, freelancing, or earning income through technology will also benefit. However, the Year of the Horse will also bring more extra expenses, so it is necessary to manage finances wisely, increase income and reduce expenditure, and store up resources for unforeseen circumstances. Although career prospects are on the rise, the clash with Tai Sui will affect interpersonal relationships. Furthermore, the presence of the inauspicious stars "Gossip" and "Strangling Star" suggests that even with good relationships with superiors and elders, attention must be paid to interactions with subordinates and colleagues to avoid gossip and even malicious attacks. Simultaneously, the destructive "Peach Blossom Star" reminds those born in the Year of the Rabbit to be cautious, avoiding unnecessary emotional entanglements in the workplace that could lead to accusations or involvement in complex personnel disputes, hindering their otherwise positive career progress. Overall, the support of benefactors in the Year of the Horse will aid career development and may even bring opportunities for promotion. However, as the saying goes, "Success comes from secrecy, failure from leaks," so it's best to avoid being overly flamboyant before things are certain, lest excessive display of talent arouse jealousy and make one vulnerable to sabotage. Therefore, it is advised that those born in the Year of the Rabbit maintain their positions diligently in the Year of the Horse, actively pursue further education to enhance their value, remain humble and modest in their interactions with others, strive to protect themselves, and remember to maintain harmonious interpersonal relationships. This will ensure upward progress in the Year of the Horse.
financial: The new year will bring the auspicious stars "Tian De," "Fu Xing," and "Ba Zuo," allowing those born in the Year of the Rabbit to receive support from many benefactors. Under the favor of fortune, the Year of the Horse will bring numerous opportunities to make money, and their wealth is expected to gradually improve. "Fu Xing" and "Ba Zuo" both represent lucky wealth; this year, small profits can be made through inspiration or intuition in investments. However, since these are not based on professional analysis and research, it is advisable to invest only small amounts for entertainment and avoid large investments. At the same time, in the ever-changing investment market, it is important to take profits promptly to prevent initial gains followed by losses. Furthermore, business owners, blessed by the auspicious star "Tian De," can fully utilize their network to expand business opportunities. However, in a year of breaking with Tai Sui, extra caution is needed in all actions. It is best to remain within familiar areas and avoid large-scale expansion. Pay close attention to the financial situation of clients and avoid extending credit or allowing them to sell on credit, lest they suffer losses due to the other party's cash flow problems. Regarding the influence of "Tongue Twister" and "Strangling Star," special attention should be paid to changes in relationships with partners. Responsibilities and obligations should be clearly defined, and any doubts should be discussed frankly to prevent financial issues from affecting good working relationships. With the arrival of the "Heavenly Joy" star, there will be more opportunities to attend banquets in the Year of the Horse, and additional expenses may arise due to marriage, the birth of a child, or renovations. However, these are considered "joyful financial losses," which those born in the Year of the Rabbit can accept generously. However, due to the influence of "Peach Blossom Star" and "Strangling Star," there is a risk of financial loss due to romantic entanglements. Those born in the Year of the Rabbit need to clarify their relationships with the opposite sex to avoid unnecessary troubles caused by financial disputes.
health: In the Year of the Horse, physical health may seem relatively good. However, influenced by the auspicious stars "Tianxi" and "Xianchi Peach Blossom," social activities and dining out will increase, necessitating the development of healthy lifestyle habits, effective weight management, and maintaining a regular sleep schedule while avoiding overeating. Those who enjoy alcohol should consume in moderation, and everything should be done in moderation to prevent digestive issues from negatively impacting health. Furthermore, the presence of "Jiaosha" indicates a need to pay special attention to respiratory issues and skin allergies. Those with allergies should be especially cautious to prevent conditions from worsening. As this is a year of breaking with Tai Sui, there will be many trivial matters and complex interpersonal relationships, which may affect emotions. Fortunately, the auspicious stars "Fuxing" and "Bazuo" will counteract these troubles, allowing those born in the Year of the Horse to quickly adjust their mindset and face challenges with optimism. If experiencing mild stress, more exercise or spending time in nature can help relieve tension. If you plan to relocate during the Year of the Horse, this can mitigate the impact. Otherwise, you can replace sofas, bedding, or curtains, and wear dog-shaped zodiac ornaments to protect yourself with the power of "Six Harmonies." After the Beginning of Spring, properly arrange the worship of Tai Sui (the Grand Duke Jupiter) to reduce adverse effects. In addition to paying attention to your own health, you should also pay more attention to the health of your elders during the Year of the Horse. It is recommended to carry out some small-scale renovations and repairs to their residences, and replace carpets, cushions, and other items that carry personal fortune. Doing more acts of medical charity and medicine donations can also improve the fortune of the home.
relationship: This year, romance blossoms, and love life is vibrant. The auspicious "Heavenly Joy" star, symbolizing numerous happy events, enters the year, significantly enhancing the charm of singles. Through introductions from elders or connections within social circles, there's a chance to meet an ideal partner, with relationships developing rapidly. There's even a possibility of impulsive marriage or double happiness. Those hoping to find a partner should seize this opportunity. However, because the Year of the Horse is prone to happy events, those not yet ready to start a family or have children should plan carefully to prevent unplanned pregnancies that could spiral out of control. Furthermore, the presence of the "Salt Pond Peach Blossom" star indicates the possibility of fleeting, illusory romances. Coupled with the difficult-to-manage "Strangling Sha" relationship, it's crucial to spend time getting to know each other before making any decisions and avoid hasty actions. If a relationship is just beginning and not yet mature, it's essential to protect it discreetly. Avoid rushing into publicizing the relationship or integrating into the partner's family and social circles to prevent outside gossip from shaking trust or the appearance of a third party that could alter the relationship. For married individuals, this is a favorable year for pursuing happy events, and those planning to have a child can begin the process, with a good chance of fulfilling their wishes. However, after successfully conceiving, it is essential to follow traditional customs and wait until three months have passed before announcing the good news to avoid any disruptions to the celebration. As for those not planning to have children, they must resolutely resist external temptations and avoid being overly enthusiastic towards the opposite sex to prevent extramarital affairs that could damage the trust and relationship they have established with their partner.

It is recommended to thoroughly understand market changes and trends before implementation and to prepare corresponding strategies in advance to avoid large capital investments. Those born in 1951 should be particularly cautious, as they are currently experiencing a period of financial loss and should not easily embark on new investment plans. Those born in 1975 will feel the influence of "heavenly harmony and earthly harmony" this month, potentially facing more troubles and obstacles at work, and some trivial problems may arise at home. Therefore, patience is needed to resolve these issues.

Career-wise, there's been new progress. If you can fully utilize your expertise at work, it's advisable to proactively strive for good performance, which may lead to a slight promotion opportunity. For those born in 1975, interpersonal disputes may occur frequently, so you need to invest more time in communication and avoid being overly stubborn in your own opinions. Hand injuries are more likely this month; when participating in outdoor activities such as hiking or trekking, it's recommended to go with others to ensure safety. As for those born in 2011, financial luck is not good, and there's a tendency to spend more than you earn; therefore, you should pay special attention to your financial management strategies.

Health warnings are emerging, especially for those with sensitive stomachs and digestive systems. It's advisable to avoid raw, cold, and greasy foods. Furthermore, when traveling, be aware of potential issues with changes in environment and climate; therefore, it's recommended to carry some medication for safety. Those born in 1963 will experience poor financial luck and should avoid making hasty investments based on dubious information, as this could lead to losses. Those born in 1987 may experience mood swings and overthinking; it's suggested they meet up with friends to confide in each other and alleviate their worries.

Those born in 1951 are prone to verbal disputes and are easily attacked by malicious individuals. Therefore, they should avoid mediating conflicts to prevent being criticized for their efforts going unrewarded. Interpersonal relationships may deteriorate, even with old friends; caution is advised in speech and behavior to avoid misunderstandings and conflicts. Those born in 1987 may experience eye problems such as inflammation or vision loss; therefore, it is recommended to schedule a professional medical examination for safety.

Learning is progressing smoothly, which helps improve one's knowledge and abilities. Therefore, one might consider enrolling in in-service training courses or other relevant interest classes to broaden one's horizons. For those born in 1963, relationships with family members are somewhat strained, often leading to cold wars over trivial matters. More communication and listening to the opinions of younger family members are important; being too domineering is not advisable. As for young people born in 1999, facing immense work pressure and poor sleep quality, they should be especially careful to avoid accidental injuries due to lack of concentration, particularly to the head and hands.

This month is auspicious for outdoor activities; short trips are suggested to "borrow luck from the land," which may improve overall fortune. Those born in 1975 should be particularly careful about financial losses and should carefully review their financial strategies, avoiding reckless spending. Foot injuries are common this month, so extra caution is needed during outdoor activities. As for those born in 2011, they may feel confused when facing complex interpersonal issues. When disagreements arise, calm communication is crucial, and harmony should always be prioritized.

During this month of conflicting elements (metal and wood), special attention must be paid to potential sprains or falls. Traffic rules must be strictly followed when crossing the street, and drivers should constantly monitor road conditions to prevent minor collisions. Those born in 1951 have relatively weaker throat and tracheal resistance, making them prone to persistent coughs; therefore, it is not recommended to consume excessive amounts of raw, cold, or spicy foods. Those born in 1987 will face financial difficulties and need to live within their means to avoid economic hardship.

According to traditional beliefs, dynamic activities are more ideal for months that clash with the zodiac sign, while static activities are not advisable. Therefore, most people born in the Year of the Pig will experience significant fluctuations in their fortunes this month. If you have plans to move this month, you can proactively embrace the changes; otherwise, it is recommended to travel more, and good fortune will naturally follow. For those born in 1963, this month is a "clash between heaven and earth," and the family will face many trivial troubles. It is recommended to carry out some renovations or repairs, or even consider replacing furniture, to improve their fortune. Those born in 1999 may feel depressed and gloomy. In such cases, it is advisable to go for a walk in the countryside with friends and use the power of nature to adjust negative emotions.

In this month of abundant romantic opportunities, single individuals should pay closer attention to those around them, as they will have the chance to meet suitable potential partners. However, it is crucial to avoid rushing into new relationships; take the time to observe and get to know each other to prevent short-lived and unstable connections. Those born in 1939 are particularly vulnerable to being deceived; if friends request financial loans, they must consult with family members to avoid innocent financial losses. Furthermore, those born in 1963 may experience a decline in family fortune; they should pay closer attention to the health of elders and accompany them to the doctor as soon as any symptoms are observed.

Your fortunes are improving across the board, with the support of benefactors allowing you to move forward and make progress in both your career and finances. You may consider small-scale investments, hoping for some modest returns; however, remember to take profits when you can to avoid losses later. For those born in 1975, unnecessary emotional troubles are particularly likely. Married individuals should especially avoid showing excessive enthusiasm towards the opposite sex to prevent misunderstandings and damage to their reputation. Those born in 1999 should be careful with their ankles; sports enthusiasts especially need to be vigilant and avoid going out alone.

Interpersonal relationships seem to have deteriorated, with conflicts frequently arising from trivial matters when interacting with family and friends. Careful wording is also necessary in the workplace to avoid being misunderstood and getting caught in a vortex of gossip. For those born in 1951, relatives and friends may request financial loans; it is advised to act within one's means and be prepared for the possibility of non-repayment. Those born in 1975 have weaker family fortunes and should pay more attention to the health of elderly relatives. This month also requires vigilance against lawsuits; pay special attention to the details of documents and contracts.

Your financial fortune will fluctuate. You are expected to recover a long-overdue debt, but you will also face more extra expenses, so you need to manage your finances carefully and plan your finances well. For those born in 1963, you will have the support of benefactors, making your work twice as effective. It is recommended that you cultivate your interpersonal relationships to advance your career to a higher level. For children born in 2011, you will often experience minor illnesses such as colds. You need to pay special attention to your daily routine and avoid scheduling too many social activities.
